title: hesperostipa neomexicana
intro: Hesperostipa neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico needle grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a plant used in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hesperostipa neomexicana include treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
